files_to_change.change: Add a nil-in-production, test-only package-level hook var `unlockOrderHook func(nodeID uint64)`, invoked synchronously inside Unlock exactly between the mu.Unlock() call and the releaseLatch() call. Extend Unlock's doc comment to reference the new deterministic regression test by name.
files_to_change.risk: low -- adds one nil-checked function-pointer call on the Unlock path, same pattern/cost as optimisticReadHook/crabRetryHook already on hot paths in this package.

files_to_change.change: Add TestNodeLatchUnlockOrderingPreventsDoubleLock: uses unlockOrderHook to pause a real Unlock() call at the documented boundary, races a concurrent Lock() call into that window, and asserts that either the same latch object is reused (safe reacquisition) or, if a different object was created (eviction path), the original object's mutex is provably already unlocked -- otherwise fails with an explicit double-lock diagnosis.
files_to_change.risk: none -- test-only file; adds one new test function, does not modify existing tests.

behavioral_change: none in production code path -- unlockOrderHook is nil in production, so the added call is a single non-blocking nil check with no side effect. No change to Lock/Unlock/TryLock/Version semantics, no change to eviction policy.
regression_risk: very low -- mirrors an established, already-proven-safe hook pattern (optimisticReadHook, optimisticRetryHook, crabRetryHook) used elsewhere in this exact package.
